pattern: Stably split a list so all nodes less than x precede all nodes greater-or-equal to x, preserving original relative order within each side.
hints: You only need two groups: nodes with value below x, and the rest — and within each group the nodes must keep the order they appeared.
Build two separate chains in one pass, appending each node to the 'less' chain or the 'ge' chain as you encounter it.
After the pass, link the tail of the 'less' chain to the head of the 'ge' chain, and remember to terminate the 'ge' chain's tail with null.

edge_inputs.description: relative order preserved within each side
value equal to x goes to the second group
all nodes already less than x

stress.description: long random list with x inside the value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, min(size, 200))
    arr = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    x = rng.randint(-100, 100)
    return [arr, x]
stress.note: stable partition gives a unique answer regardless of x

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def partition(self, head, x):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        less = [v for v in vals if v < x]
        ge = [v for v in vals if v >= x]
        ordered = less + ge
        out = None
        for v in reversed(ordered):
            out = ListNode(v, out)
        return out

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def partition(self, head, x):
        less_head = ListNode(0)
        ge_head = ListNode(0)
        less = less_head
        ge = ge_head
        node = head
        while node:
            if node.val < x:
                less.next = node
                less = less.next
            else:
                ge.next = node
                ge = ge.next
            node = node.next
        ge.next = None
        less.next = ge_head.next
        return less_head.next
